mission.title: Install and Configure MySQL Operator for Kubernetes (Oracle)
mission.description: The MySQL Operator for Kubernetes automates the management of MySQL InnoDB Cluster setups within Kubernetes, handling lifecycle tasks such as deployment, upgrades, and backups. It is ideal for users looking to simplify MySQL database management in a Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and the potential complexity of managing database configurations.

steps.title: Add the MySQL Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official MySQL Operator Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the operator easily.

```bash

helm repo add mysql-operator https://mysql.github.io/mysql-operator/

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the MySQL Operator repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the MySQL Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the MySQL Operator in a dedicated namespace, which is necessary for managing MySQL clusters.

```bash

helm install mysql-operator mysql-operator/mysql-operator --namespace mysql-operator --create-namespace --version 9.0.0

```

> This command installs the MySQL Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a secret for MySQL root user
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes secret that stores the credentials for the MySQL root user, which is required for administrative tasks.

```bash

kubectl create secret generic mypwds --from-literal=rootUser=root --from-literal=rootHost=% --from-literal=rootPassword: <REDACTED>' --namespace mysql-operator

```

> Replace 'sakila' with your desired root password.
commands: kubectl create secret generic mypwds --from-literal=rootUser=root --from-literal=rootHost=% --from-literal=rootPassword: <REDACTED>' --namespace mysql-operator

steps.title: Deploy a MySQL InnoDB Cluster
steps.description:
This step defines and deploys a MySQL InnoDB Cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - --namespace mysql-operator <<EOF
apiVersion: mysql.oracle.com/v2
kind: InnoDBCluster
metadata:
  name: mycluster
spec:
  secretName: mypwds
  version: '8.0.26'
EOF

```

> This command creates a MySQL InnoDB Cluster named 'mycluster'.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - --namespace mysql-operator <<EOF
apiVersion: mysql.oracle.com/v2
kind: InnoDBCluster
metadata:
  name: mycluster
spec:
  secretName: mypwds
  version: '8.0.26'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the MySQL Operator is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the MySQL Operator deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get deployment -n mysql-operator mysql-operator

```

> You should see the deployment status as '1/1' under 'READY'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MySQL Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MySQL Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall mysql-operator --namespace mysql-operator

kubectl delete namespace mysql-operator

```

> This command uninstalls the MySQL Operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MySQL Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your MySQL data. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the deployment afterward.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n mysql-operator -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade mysql-operator mysql-operator/mysql-operator --namespace mysql-operator --version 9.0.1

kubectl get deployment -n mysql-operator mysql-operator

```

> Drains all pods in the mysql-operator namespace.
> This command upgrades the MySQL Operator to the specified version.
> Verify the deployment status after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n mysql-operator -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create resource: the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the CRD for InnoDBCluster is not installed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mysql/mysql-operator/9.0.0/deploy/deploy-crds.yaml' to install the CRD.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'MySQL Operator is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MySQL Operator deployment may have failed due to insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs -n mysql-operator <pod-name>' and ensure your cluster meets the resource requirements.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Secret mypwds not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The secret containing MySQL credentials has not been created or is in the wrong namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure the secret is created in the 'mysql-operator' namespace using the correct command.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to MySQL server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the MySQL InnoDB Cluster is not ready.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get innodbclusters -n mysql-operator' to check the status of the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for MySQL 8.0.26, improved backup and restore functionalities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from version 8.4, ensure to update your MySQL configuration files to align with the new version requirements.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get innodbclusters -n mysql-operator' showing the cluster status as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: kubectl create secret generic mypwds --from-literal=rootUser=root --from-literal=rootHost=% --from-literal=rootPassword: <REDACTED>' --namespace mysql-operator

kubectl apply -f - --namespace mysql-operator <<EOF
apiVersion: mysql.oracle.com/v2
kind: InnoDBCluster
metadata:
  name: mycluster
spec:
  secretName: mypwds
  version: '8.0.26'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
